name: Machine Starter
description:
Day-one program for true beginners and older adults. All machines, fixed movement paths, low cognitive load. Two rotating full body workouts, 2-3x/week.

Program starts with lower volume (sets) and intensity and gradually ramps up. Lower intensity for very taxing exercises like Leg Press - gradually adjust to hard exercises.

weeks.description: Your first week. Everything is light on purpose so you can learn each machine and find weights that work for you. Focus on smooth, controlled reps.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Feet high on the pad is easier. Feet low requires greater flexibility and strength. Don't lock out knees at the top. Control the eccentric.

exercises.name: Dip Machine
exercises.notes: Lean slightly forward for chest emphasis, stay upright for tricep emphasis.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Feet high on the pad is easier. Feet low requires greater flexibility and strength. Don't lock out knees at the top. Control the eccentric.

exercises.name: Thigh Abductor
exercises.notes: Lean forward during sets to activate glutes.

weeks.description: Same exercises, a bit more effort. You know the machines now, so push a little harder on each set.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Feet high on the pad is easier. Feet low requires greater flexibility and strength. Don't lock out knees at the top. Control the eccentric.

exercises.name: Dip Machine
exercises.notes: Lean slightly forward for chest emphasis, stay upright for tricep emphasis.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Feet high on the pad is easier. Feet low requires greater flexibility and strength. Don't lock out knees at the top. Control the eccentric.

exercises.name: Thigh Abductor
exercises.notes: Lean forward during sets to activate glutes.

weeks.description: Staying at this intensity to let your body adapt. Consistency matters more than going heavy right now.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Feet high on the pad is easier. Feet low requires greater flexibility and strength. Don't lock out knees at the top. Control the eccentric.

exercises.name: Dip Machine
exercises.notes: Lean slightly forward for chest emphasis, stay upright for tricep emphasis.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Feet high on the pad is easier. Feet low requires greater flexibility and strength. Don't lock out knees at the top. Control the eccentric.

exercises.name: Thigh Abductor
exercises.notes: Lean forward during sets to activate glutes.

weeks.description: Your hardest week. Leave less in the tank on each set and see what you can do.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Feet high on the pad is easier. Feet low requires greater flexibility and strength. Don't lock out knees at the top. Control the eccentric.

exercises.name: Dip Machine
exercises.notes: Lean slightly forward for chest emphasis, stay upright for tricep emphasis.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Feet high on the pad is easier. Feet low requires greater flexibility and strength. Don't lock out knees at the top. Control the eccentric.

exercises.name: Thigh Abductor
exercises.notes: Lean forward during sets to activate glutes.

weeks.name: Deload Week
weeks.description: Recovery week. Lighter effort to let your muscles catch up to the work you put in.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Feet high on the pad is easier. Feet low requires greater flexibility and strength. Don't lock out knees at the top. Control the eccentric.

exercises.name: Dip Machine
exercises.notes: Lean slightly forward for chest emphasis, stay upright for tricep emphasis.

exercises.name: Leg Press
